# Astro 5.x — Tech Pack

## 1. Fingerprinting Signals

| Signal | Type | Value | Confidence |
|--------|------|-------|------------|
| `/_astro/` paths in HTML `<link>` and `<script>` | HTML substring | `/_astro/` | Definitive |
| `<meta name="generator" content="Astro v5` | HTML meta | `Astro v5` | Version |
| `x-astro-version` response header | HTTP header | semver string | Version |
| `.astro` file paths in source maps | Source map | `.astro` extension | High |
| Wappalyzer result | MCP | `"Astro"` | Definitive |

**Version extraction:**
```bash
curl -s {site} | grep -oP 'content="Astro v\K[0-9.]+'
# Or from response header:
curl -sI {site} | grep -i x-astro-version
```

## 2. Default API Surfaces

| Endpoint | Method | Auth | Notes |
|----------|--------|------|-------|
| `/api/*` | GET/POST | Varies | API routes in `src/pages/api/` (SSR mode only) |
| `/_image` | GET | None | Built-in image optimisation endpoint |
| `/_astro/*.js` | GET | None | Compiled JS chunks |
| `/_astro/*.css` | GET | None | Compiled CSS chunks |
| `/sitemap-index.xml` | GET | None | If `@astrojs/sitemap` integration used |
| `/rss.xml` | GET | None | Common RSS feed path (manual route) |

**Note:** API routes only exist when Astro is in SSR (`output: "server"` or `"hybrid"`) mode. Static output (`output: "static"`) has no runtime API surface.

## 3. Config / Constants Locations

| Location | What's there | How to access |
|----------|-------------|---------------|
| `astro.config.mjs` | Integrations, output mode, base URL, adapter | Source only |
| `/_astro/*.js` bundles | Client-side globals, content collection schemas | JS parse/eval |
| `window.__ASTRO__` | Internal Astro client runtime object | JS eval in browser |
| `import.meta.env.*` | Environment variables (public ones with `PUBLIC_` prefix only) | JS eval (`import.meta.env`) |
| Content collections JSON | Structured content entries | `/_content/` or bundle |

## 4. Auth Patterns

Astro has no built-in auth. Common patterns depend on chosen library:

| Pattern | Library | Detection |
|---------|---------|-----------|
| Session cookies | `@astrojs/auth` / Lucia / Auth.js | `session` or `auth-token` cookie |
| JWT Bearer | Custom middleware | `Authorization` header on API routes |
| Clerk | `@clerk/astro` | `__session` cookie; Clerk JS in bundles |
| Supabase | `@supabase/ssr` | `sb-` prefixed cookies |
| Netlify Identity | `netlify-identity-widget` | `gotrue-session` cookie |

## 5. JS Bundle Patterns

| Path | Content |
|------|---------|
| `/_astro/[hash].js` | Compiled component JS (hashed filename) |
| `/_astro/[hash].css` | Compiled styles |
| `/_astro/client.js` | Astro client runtime (view transitions etc.) |
| `/src/` paths in source maps | Original `.astro`, `.tsx`, `.ts` component sources |

**Finding bundle URLs:**
```bash
# Extract all /_astro/ asset URLs from HTML
curl -s {site} | grep -oP '/_astro/[^"'\'']+\.(js|css)'
```

## 6. Source Map Patterns

Astro production builds strip source maps by default. Development mode (`astro dev`) includes them.

- Check `/_astro/[hash].js.map` — usually 404 in production
- If source maps present: reveals original file paths including `.astro` component names
- `sourceMappingURL=` comment at end of JS bundle indicates source map availability

## 7. Common Plugins & Extensions

| Integration | What it adds | Detection signal |
|-------------|-------------|-----------------|
| `@astrojs/react` | React components | `react` in bundles |
| `@astrojs/vue` | Vue components | `vue` in bundles |
| `@astrojs/svelte` | Svelte components | `svelte` in bundles |
| `@astrojs/tailwind` | Tailwind CSS | `tailwind` classes in HTML |
| `@astrojs/sitemap` | `/sitemap-index.xml` | Sitemap at standard path |
| `@astrojs/mdx` | MDX content support | `.mdx` in source map paths |
| `@astrojs/db` | Astro DB (libSQL) | `/_db/` routes or `astro:db` in bundles |
| Vercel adapter | `/api/` serverless functions | Vercel-specific headers |
| Netlify adapter | Edge functions | `netlify-edge` in response headers |

## 8. Known Public Data

| Endpoint | Data | Notes |
|----------|------|-------|
| `/_astro/*.js` | Client-side component code | Grep for API calls, endpoints, config |
| `/sitemap-index.xml` | All public URLs grouped by type | Use for Phase 6 URL discovery |
| `/rss.xml` | Content feed with item titles and URLs | If blog-style site |
| `window.__ASTRO__` (browser eval) | Client router state, prefetch config | Phase 11 JS globals |
| `import.meta.env` (browser eval) | Public env vars (`PUBLIC_*` prefix only) | Phase 11 JS globals |

## 9. Probe Checklist

- [ ] `GET /` — check for `/_astro/` assets and generator meta; extract version
- [ ] `HEAD /_image` — confirm image optimisation endpoint active (SSR indicator)
- [ ] `GET /api/` — probe for API route index (SSR mode)
- [ ] `GET /sitemap-index.xml` — if present, download all sitemap URLs for Phase 2
- [ ] `GET /rss.xml` — common content feed
- [ ] Extract all `/_astro/*.js` bundle URLs from HTML; download largest bundle for endpoint grep
- [ ] Grep largest bundle for: `fetch(`, `axios.`, `/api/`, `PUBLIC_` env var names
- [ ] Check `/_astro/*.js.map` on one bundle — 200 means source maps enabled (rare in prod)
- [ ] `GET /robots.txt` — may reveal content paths not linked from homepage
- [ ] Check response headers for adapter-specific signals (`x-vercel-*`, `netlify-*`)

## 10. Gotchas

- **Static vs SSR distinction is critical.** A static Astro site has zero runtime API surface — all "API calls" happen at build time. Look for `/_image` or any server response that isn't a static file to confirm SSR.
- **No single-page app routing by default.** Astro uses MPA (multi-page) navigation unless `<ViewTransitions />` is used. Network capture in Phase 11 will show full page navigations, not XHR-only API calls.
- **Content Collections are build-time only.** Content Collections compile to JSON at build time and are bundled into JS — they don't create runtime HTTP endpoints.
- **Hashed filenames change per build.** `/_astro/` asset paths include a content hash. Never hardcode them; always extract from HTML.
- **`import.meta.env` leaks only `PUBLIC_` vars.** Astro strips all private env vars from client bundles. Only variables prefixed `PUBLIC_` are accessible in browser JS eval.
- **Islands architecture means partial hydration.** Only interactive components ship JS. A content page may have zero client JS despite a rich UI.

## 11. GitHub Code Search Patterns

Use these queries on GitHub to find custom endpoints, plugin code, and configuration examples for this framework.

### Framework-Specific Queries

| Search Query | What it finds |
|--------------|---------------|
| `"export function GET" language:typescript path:src/pages/api/` | Astro API endpoint definitions |
| `"Astro.request" language:typescript` | Astro request object usage |
| `"getStaticPaths" language:typescript path:src/pages/` | Static route generation patterns |
| `"Astro.cookies" language:typescript` | Cookie handling in Astro components |

### Example Queries for Astro

```bash
# Search for custom API routes/endpoints
site:github.com "Astro" "api" filetype:ts "export function GET"

# Search for authentication patterns
site:github.com "Astro" "auth" "middleware" language:typescript

# Search for configuration files with endpoint definitions
site:github.com "Astro" "astro.config" "endpoint" language:javascript

# Search for custom post types, taxonomies, or extensions
site:github.com "Astro" "content collection" "custom" language:typescript
```

## 12. Framework-Specific Google Dorks

Use these Google search queries to discover exposed endpoints, configuration files, and documentation for this framework.

### Discovery Queries

| Search Query | What it finds |
|--------------|---------------|
| `site:{domain} inurl:/_astro/` | Astro compiled JS/CSS assets with content hashes |
| `site:{domain} inurl:__ASTRO__` | Astro client runtime global objects |
| `site:{domain} inurl:/api/` | Astro SSR API routes |
| `site:{domain} "Astro" "endpoint"` | Astro API endpoint references in content |

### Complete Dork List for Astro

```
# API endpoints
site:{domain} inurl:/api/
site:{domain} inurl:/_image
site:{domain} inurl:/sitemap-index.xml

# Framework-specific paths
site:{domain} inurl:/_astro/
site:{domain} inurl:__ASTRO__

# Configuration files
site:{domain} filetype:js "astro.config"
site:{domain} filetype:json "import.meta.env"

# Documentation/leaks
site:{domain} "Astro" "api" "endpoint"
site:{domain} "PUBLIC_" "astro"

# Admin/debug paths
site:{domain} inurl:/api/
site:{domain} inurl:/_astro/
```

## 13. Cross-Cutting OSINT Patterns

These patterns apply across frameworks and should be checked for any detected technology.

### Favicon Hashing

Identify technology stack by hashing favicon and searching Shodan/Censys for same stack:

```bash
# Get favicon hash (mmh3 hash of favicon content)
curl -s "https://{domain}/favicon.ico" | python3 -c "
import sys, hashlib, base64
data = sys.stdin.buffer.read()
# Simple mmh3 hash simulation using Python
import mmh3 2>/dev/null || pip install mmh3
# Or use: python3 -c "import mmh3; print(mmh3.hash(data))"
print('Favicon hash needed for Shodan search: icon_hash')
"

# Search Shodan for same favicon (indicates shadow IT subdomains)
# site:shodan.io search: icon_hash:{hash}
```

**What it reveals:** Hidden subdomains running same framework stack as main site.

### Source Map Discovery

Check for source maps across all JS bundles:

```bash
# Extract all JS bundle URLs from HTML
curl -s "https://{domain}/" | grep -oP 'src="[^"]+\.js[^"]*"' | grep -oP '"[^"]+' | tr -d '"' > js_urls.txt

# Check each for .map file
while read url; do
  map_url="${url}.map"
  status=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "${map_url}")
  [ "$status" = "200" ] && echo "SOURCE MAP: ${map_url}"
done < js_urls.txt
```

**Build tool patterns:**
| Build Tool | Source Map Pattern | Detection |
|------------|-------------------|------------|
| Webpack | `{bundle}.js.map` or `//# sourceMappingURL=` | Check response header `X-SourceMap` |
| Vite | `{name}-[hash].js.map` | Vite manifest `manifest.json` |
| Rollup | `{bundle}.js.map` | Check `sourceMappingURL` comment |
| esbuild | `{bundle}.js.map` | Check `sourceMappingURL` comment |
| Next.js | `/_next/static/chunks/*.js.map` | Only if `productionBrowserSourceMaps: true` |

### Tech Stack → API Pattern Mapping

Auto-map detected frameworks to likely endpoint patterns:

| Framework | Common API Patterns |
|-----------|---------------------|
| Next.js | `/api/*`, `/_next/data/*`, `/api/auth/*`, `/api/trpc/*` |
| WordPress | `/wp-json/*`, `/wp-json/wp/v2/*`, `/wp-admin/admin-ajax.php` |
| Shopify | `/api/2024-10/graphql.json`, `/products.json`, `/collections.json` |
| Rails | `/api/v1/*`, `/assets/*`, `/users/sign_in` |
| Laravel | `/api/*`, `/livewire/message/*`, `/sanctum/csrf-cookie` |
| Strapi | `/api/*`, `/admin/*`, `/api/upload*` |
| Magento | `/rest/V1/*`, `/pub/static/*` |
| Django | `/api/*`, `/admin/*`, `/accounts/*` |

When Phase 3 detects a framework, use this table to prioritize Phase 5/6/7 probes.

### Email Naming Convention Analysis

Extract emails from theHarvester/GitHub results to predict internal subdomains:

```bash
# Sample emails found: john.doe@example.com, jane.smith@example.com
# Predicted subdomains: mail.example.com, smtp.example.com, exchange.example.com

# Common patterns:
# first.last@ → internal.example.com, mail.example.com
# firstinitial+last@ → owa.example.com, outlook.example.com
```

**Add to Phase 9 session brief:** Note email patterns and predicted subdomains.
